The decision could reshape how aviation manufacturers and airlines assess technological failures and pilot preparedness, facing not just civil but criminal liability for systemic shortcomings.

articles.title: AI Automation Creates More Expert Work, Not Less
articles.summary: Contrary to widespread fears of mass job displacement, AI automation appears to be generating a new class of expert roles rather than simply eliminating them. New analyses suggest that while routine tasks are automated, the demand for human expertise in areas like AI oversight, strategic integration, and novel problem-solving is surging. This paradigm shift means the future workforce will require re-skilling towards higher cognitive functions, emphasizing human-AI collaboration over pure replacement, challenging the prevailing narrative of AI as a net job destroyer.

articles.title: Hanselman's 'Don't Roll Your Own' Highlights Software Pitfalls
